component.name: Format Foundry
component.summary: Cross-platform file conversion and workflow workspace

description.p: Format Foundry provides conversion, extraction, metadata editing, archive handling, media workflows, aria2 downloads, and storage-analysis tools in a single desktop application.
It is designed for desktop batch workflows where converting, packaging, extracting, downloading, and reviewing outputs need to happen in one place.

screenshot.caption: Format Foundry workspace on Linux desktop

ul.li: The updater now accepts GitHub release asset SHA256 digests so checksum verification works against the canonical release feed.
Linux startup animation now uses a Linux-safe splash window mode so the intro animation appears reliably on desktop launches.
Added a collapsible overview shell so the large startup header and quick actions can be hidden when more workspace area is needed.
Reorganized the top menu into File, Edit, Settings, Overview, and Help for more direct access to shell controls.
Improved Linux readability with adaptive scaling, Linux-friendly fonts, and scrollable module content that keeps bottom controls reachable.
Added structured support diagnostics, backend version reporting, and exportable bug reports for long-term support and troubleshooting.
Fixed Linux first-run setup so action buttons remain visible and keyboard shortcuts can complete the wizard.
Expanded AppStream metadata with release notes, screenshot data, content rating, and license-status links for software centers.
Fixed Linux packaged startup visibility so the app no longer risks starting without a visible window.
Improved coordinated Windows and Linux release delivery.
Added Debian and AppImage Linux distribution support.
Expanded aria2 downloads, torrent workflows, and cross-platform UI improvements.
